title: armeria elongata var. sibirica
intro:
Armeria elongata var. sibirica, also known as Sibirian Thistle or Sibirisk Ballmunk, is a flowering plant species in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Siberia and the Caucasus.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Armeria elongata var. sibirica include its ability to soothe sore throats, ease congestion, and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used internally to treat various health conditions.
